title: PropWatch Configuration
description: Configuration file for PropWatch — detects test/prop ID changes in CI pipelines.

baseRef.description: Git ref to compare the current HEAD against.

exitOnBreaking.description: Exit with a non-zero code when breaking changes are detected.

reporters.description: Reporters to use when outputting results.

outputDir.description: Directory where file-based reporters write their output.

autoDetect.description: Automatically detect packages in the monorepo.

packages.description: Explicit list of package paths to scan. Set to null to use autoDetect.

reportStrategy.description: How to report results across packages.

patterns.description: Patterns defining what PropWatch looks for in your source files.

ignorePatterns.description: Glob patterns for files to exclude from scanning.

fileGlobs.description: Glob patterns selecting which files this pattern applies to.

parser.description: Parser to use for extracting values. Use 'regex' for simple attribute matching or 'babel' for full AST traversal.

propPatterns.description: Regex patterns (with a capture group) used by the regex parser to extract values.

propNames.description: JSX attribute names to extract, used by the babel parser.

astOptions.description: Options passed directly to @babel/parser. Overrides the defaults set by PropWatch.

junitProperties.description: Key/value pairs emitted as <property> elements inside each JUnit <testcase> for this pattern. Not included by default — only add when your CI platform requires them. Example: Bitbucket reads 'bbc.summary.kind' to surface results in the Tests tab.
